annotation_info.name: Annotation submitted by Royal Botanic Gardens Kew

bioprojects.title: Rhizophagus irregularis strain:DAOM197198 Genome sequencing and assembly

description.comment: This work presents the most contiguous and highest-quality nuclear and mitochondrial genome assemblies of an arbuscular mycorrhizal fungus to date, achieved through Nanopore long-read DNA sequencing and Hi-C data. This genome assembly, alongside short- and long-read RNA-Sequencing data, was used to produce a comprehensive annotation catalogue of gene models, repetitive elements, small RNA loci, and DNA cytosine methylome.
description.title: Rhizophagus irregularis DAOM-197198 chromosome-scale genome assembly

bioprojects.title: In-depth phylogenomic analysis of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi based on a comprehensive set of de novo genome assemblies

attributes.name: sequencing method
attributes.value: Illumina, HiSeqX, paired-end 150bp read_length, v2.5 sequencing chemistry

assembly_info.comments: All sets of reads from individually sequenced nuclei were combined and normalized using bbnorm of BBMap v.38.08 (Bushnell, 2014), setting an average depth of 100X, and then assembled using SPAdes v.3.12.0 (Bankevich et al., 2012).
